name: The First National Bank of Anson
description_short: Community bank operating in Anson, Texas since 1902, offering traditional checking, savings, loans, mortgages, and online banking services.
description_long: First National Bank of Anson was chartered by the U.S. Treasury Department on January 11, 1902, and remains the oldest continuously operating bank in Jones County under its original charter and name. The bank survived the Great Depression without reorganization and acquired assets from three failed competing banks (Jones County Bank in 1902, Farmers and Merchants National Bank in 1915, and First State Bank in 1930). Leadership has emphasized strong management succession, with Mitch Heidenheimer currently serving as Chairman, President, and CEO following a control group purchase led by Jim Heidenheimer in 1977. The bank offers a comprehensive range of financial products including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), individual retirement accounts (IRAs), personal and commercial loans, mortgages, safe deposit boxes, mobile banking, and internet banking. Their physical location is at 1012 12th Street in Anson, Texas, with drive-through service available Monday through Friday, 8:30 AM to 3:00 PM. The bank emphasizes competitive loan and deposit rates and community involvement through its employees' participation in church offices, booster clubs, and local activities. What distinguishes First National Bank of Anson is its deep historical roots and continuity in a single market. Being the only bank in Anson to survive the Depression era without reorganization, combined with over 120 years of operation under the same charter and name, demonstrates institutional stability. The bank explicitly focuses on community-based financial services with a stated mission to provide positive and lasting impact on customers, employees, and surrounding areas. As a small community bank serving primarily Jones County and surrounding areas, it is best suited for customers seeking personalized, relationship-based banking from an established local institution. However, customers should note that as a regional bank with limited physical and digital infrastructure compared to larger national banks, services may be more limited in scope and technological sophistication. The bank's 3:00 PM closing time (even earlier than many regional banks) may inconvenience working professionals.
pros: Over 120 years of continuous operation under original charter—only bank in Jones County to survive the Great Depression without reorganization
Acquired three failed banks' assets, demonstrating stability and growth throughout economic downturns
Community-focused mission with employees actively involved in local church, civic, and booster club activities
Full-service banking offerings including checking, savings, CDs, IRAs, mortgages, personal loans, and safe deposit boxes
Online and mobile banking platforms for remote account management
Competitive loan and deposit rates explicitly mentioned as part of their service model
Routing number 111304608 confirms legitimate federal charter status
cons: Limited geographic footprint—primarily serves Jones County and immediate surrounding areas, not suitable for customers needing multi-state banking
Early closing time of 3:00 PM on weekdays may be inconvenient for working professionals
No information provided about minimum account balances, fees, or specific rate competitiveness data
Appears to have limited digital infrastructure compared to national or regional banks—no mention of mobile app features, 24/7 customer service, or advanced fintech tools
Small-town bank may lack specialized services (investment advisory, wealth management, business banking complexity) available at larger institutions
best_for: Jones County residents and business owners seeking long-term relationship banking with a locally-rooted institution
Customers prioritizing community involvement and local reinvestment over broader product selection
Individuals and small businesses comfortable with traditional banking services and limited digital functionality
Homebuyers in rural Texas seeking mortgage products from a lender with deep local market knowledge
diagnosis: First National Bank of Anson is best for Jones County and regional residents seeking stable, community-focused banking from an institution with proven longevity through multiple economic cycles. The primary caveat is geographic and service limitation—this is a small community bank suitable for basic to intermediate banking needs, not customers requiring sophisticated financial products, national branch access, or advanced digital banking.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
fdic_official_name: The First National Bank of Anson
